2605.28740.title: Reverse Probing: Supervised Token-level Uncertainty Quantification for Large Language Models in Clinical Text
2605.28740.paper_summary: As large language models are increasingly deployed for clinical text, ensuring they can reliably signal their own uncertainty becomes critical. Most existing uncertainty quantification (UQ) methods are designed for open-domain generation and cannot localize uncertainty at the token or span level in long clinical text. We propose Reverse Probing, the first UQ framework specialized for clinical summarization, which estimates token-level uncertainty directly from pre-existing labeled summaries. Rather than sampling new outputs, Reverse Probing treats the text as a probe into the model's internal state, extracting uncertainty signals from four categories of internal activations. We evaluate on two expert-annotated clinical datasets and outperform eight adapted baselines on all metrics, achieving up to 4 times higher AUPRC while reducing inference time and computational costs. Feature analysis reveals that delta energy and neighborhood context are the most consistent predictors across all models. This study offers interpretable insights into how models internally respond to unsupported clinical content.
2605.28740.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型在臨床文本中的應用日益增多，確保它們能可靠地表達自身的不確定性變得至關重要。大多數現有的不確定性量化（UQ）方法是為開放域生成設計的，無法在長臨床文本中定位到標記或範圍級別的不確定性。我們提出了反向探測（Reverse Probing），這是第一個專門針對臨床摘要的不確定性量化框架，能夠直接從現有標記摘要中估計標記級別的不確定性。反向探測並不是採樣新的輸出，而是將文本視為對模型內部狀態的探測，從四類內部激活中提取不確定性信號。我們在兩個專家標註的臨床數據集上進行評估，在所有指標上超越了八個改編的基準，實現了高達4倍的AUPRC，同時減少推理時間和計算成本。特徵分析顯示，增量能量和鄰域上下文是所有模型中最一致的預測因子。本研究提供了可解釋的見解，揭示了模型如何在內部對不支持的臨床內容作出反應。
2605.28740.author: Bushi Xiao et.al.
2605.28740.authors: Bushi Xiao, Sarvesh Soni, Daisy Zhe Wang
